summary: Split the shared search index into per-tenant physical shards, route queries at the gateway by tenant identity, migrate existing documents, and make the old shared path unreachable post-cutover. Cross-tenant leakage incidents this quarter make this a security-adjacent refactor; UX is unchanged but SLOs must hold and a rollback is required for perf regressions on small tenants.

reversibility.why: Once the shared-index path is removed and tenants are re-sharded, reverting requires re-joining indices or restoring from backup — not a config flip.

blast_radius.why: Every tenant's search queries flow through the new gateway routing; a mis-sharded query is a cross-tenant leak on a surface that already had two incidents this quarter.

compliance_scope.why: Tenant isolation is a baseline SOC2/enterprise-contract expectation; this isn't GDPR/HIPAA territory but the leak history makes tenant data sensitivity non-negligible.

user_facing_impact.why: UX is unchanged but latency SLOs must hold; a small tenant with a tiny shard could regress on cold-cache queries and that is user-visible.

novelty.why: Per-tenant sharding is a known pattern industry-wide but this is the first time in this codebase; gateway routing + authz check between identity and shard selection is new internal machinery.

scope_effort.why: Data migration for all existing tenants' docs, gateway rewrite, authz check, shared-path removal, tenant metrics isolation, rollback scripts — many components.

state_complexity.why: Index data, per-tenant metrics, and migration cutover state must stay consistent; dual-write or dual-read windows need explicit plans.

operational_risk.why: Search is latency-critical and tenant-isolation-critical; a bad shard route or a migration-window perf regression is a runtime incident.

coordination_cost.why: Search + platform + data + product teams affected; release windows, tenant notifications, and rollback criteria need explicit sign-off.

specialists.name: solution-architect
specialists.why: Picks the shard topology and routing design, names the trade-offs between per-tenant indices vs. routing-aliases vs. virtual shards.

specialists.name: data-engineer
specialists.why: Owns the document migration, dual-write / dual-read window, and cutover plan.

specialists.name: migration-engineer
specialists.why: Designs the rollback path, verifies migrated-doc counts against source, and lays out the failure-recovery runbook.

specialists.name: security-engineer
specialists.why: Two prior cross-tenant leakage incidents make authz-between-identity-and-shard a mandatory design review topic.

specialists.name: backend-engineer
specialists.why: Implements the gateway routing, shard-selection authz check, and tenant metrics isolation.

specialists.name: test-strategist
specialists.why: Designs the tenant-isolation adversarial test suite, perf-regression suite for small tenants, and migration-parity tests.

specialists.name: sre
specialists.why: Owns latency SLOs during cutover, alert thresholds, and the rollback trigger.

specialists.name: release-engineer
specialists.why: Coordinates the staged cutover across tenant cohorts and the communication plan.

phases.name: clarify
phases.why: Name the tenant cohorts, define per-tenant SLO targets, and pin down what constitutes cross-tenant leakage in the adversarial test plan.

phases.name: design
phases.why: Pick the shard topology, gateway routing contract, authz check, migration plan, and rollback design.

phases.name: test-strategy
phases.why: Adversarial tenant-isolation scenarios + perf-regression suite + migration-parity tests MUST exist before build; rigor_tier=full requires this.

phases.name: build
phases.why: Implement gateway routing, authz check, tenant metrics isolation, migration scripts, rollback scripts; includes a migration sub-phase.

phases.name: test
phases.why: Execute adversarial isolation, perf-regression, migration-parity, and rollback-drill scenarios against a staging fleet.

phases.name: review
phases.why: Multi-reviewer sign-off: architecture, security, SRE + release. Full-rigor gates require ≥2 reviewers and compliance-traceability evidence.

rigor_why: Low reversibility, HIGH blast_radius, HIGH operational + state + coordination, plus a documented leakage history make this unambiguously full rigor — auto_proceed is ignored, acceptance requires security-scan + migration-rollback-plan + performance-baseline evidence.
complexity_why: Cross-cutting change touching data, gateway, authz, and ops simultaneously, with a compliance-adjacent leakage history and an SLO guarantee — sits at the top of the scale.
open_questions: What's the hard SLO delta (e.g., p95 latency) at which we auto-rollback vs. continue cutover?
Dual-write window vs. atomic cutover per tenant — product's preference for migration-visible disruption?
Small-tenant shard-cold-start mitigation: warm-up queries, shared-cache tier, or accepted regression during migration?
Do we retire the shared-index code path by feature flag or by delete-in-same-release?

tasks.title: Clarify tenant cohorts, SLO targets, and cross-tenant leakage definition
Design shard topology + gateway routing + authz check (ADR + security review)
Design migration + rollback plan (data engineer + migration engineer)
Write test strategy: tenant-isolation adversarial, perf-regression, migration-parity
Implement gateway routing + shard-selection authz check + tenant metrics isolation
Implement migration scripts + rollback scripts + cutover runner
Execute adversarial tenant-isolation + perf-regression + migration-parity + rollback-drill
Security-engineer review: cross-tenant leakage adversarial coverage sign-off
SRE + release-engineer review: SLO, rollout cohort plan, rollback trigger sign-off
